perturbation.original_text: Service Recipient shall pay Service Provider the [service fees]/ [commissions and service fees] ([collectively,] "Service Fee") (inclusive of all applicable taxes) in accordance with Schedule 1 for the Services provided by Service Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: Service Recipient shall pay Service Provider the [service fees]/ [commissions and service fees] ([collectively,] "Service Fee") (inclusive of all applicable taxes) in accordance with Schedule 1 for the Services provided by Service Provider, *only if Service Recipient has achieved a 20% increase in student enrollment as a direct result of the services provided.*
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a condition for payment of the Service Fee, requiring a 20% increase in student enrollment directly resulting from the services. This contradicts the original unconditional payment obligation specified in Section 4, creating uncertainty about when Service Fees are actually due, as the original agreement did not specify that payment of the Service Fee was contingent upon the company achieving any such metric and directly conflicts with the terms of the schedule 1 where the payment are defined in accordance to actual costs incurred by Service Provider in connection with its provision of the services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Service Recipient shall pay to Service Provider the Service Fee on a quarterly basis. The amount of the Service Fee shall be [determined on the basis of the actual costs incurred by Service Provider in connection with its provision of the services, plus a mark-up at a percentage as agreed upon between both Parties, to be allocated to Service Recipient and other service recipients in proportion to their respective revenues] / [computed at a percentage (as agreed upon between both Parties) of Service Recipient's revenues] and confirmed by a letter of confirmation substantially in the form attached hereto. Service Fee shall be paid after each quarter. Service Recipient shall pay the Service Fee for the preceding quarter in accordance with a written payment instruction of Service Provider during each quarter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition (20% increase in student enrollment) for payment of the Service Fee, directly contradicting the original, unconditional payment obligation stated in the 'Contradicted Text'. The original text dictates quarterly payments based on 'actual costs incurred,' while the changed text makes payment contingent on a specific performance metric. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces a condition to payment that did not previously exist, making the payment terms ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider. Service Recipient does not obtain any intellectual property rights in respect of the Services by entering into this Agreement. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider, *except for any materials specifically created by Service Recipient's employees for use in conjunction with the Services, which will be jointly owned.* Service Recipient does not obtain any intellectual property rights in respect of the Services by entering into this Agreement. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the ownership of intellectual property, stipulating that materials created by Service Recipient's employees for use with the Services are jointly owned. This directly contradicts the later statement in the same section that ALL intellectual property rights created or derived from the services, regardless of who developed or created it, shall be exclusively owned by the service provider. This creates uncertainty on which party has right to use materials made by Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider, *except for any materials specifically created by Service Recipient's employees for use in conjunction with the Services, which will be jointly owned.* Service Recipient does not obtain any intellectual property rights in respect of the Services by entering into this Agreement. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that 'all the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services...shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.' The changed text introduces an exception, stating that materials created by Service Recipient's employees for use with the Services are jointly owned. This directly contradicts the statement that ALL intellectual property rights, regardless of who developed it, are exclusively owned by the service provider. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the change creates uncertainty on which party has right to use materials made by Recipient.
